description: Transaction value entered by the user.
Product or transaction category from the source system.
Hour of the transaction, 0 to 23.
Day-of-week or weekly timing pattern. In real data this should come from the actual timestamp.
Card network or payment network where available.
Card/payment type such as debit or credit where available.
Business-safe proxy for how common/familiar the payment identifier pattern is. This is not raw card1 and should not be described as literal customer history.
Whether the main address/location signal is available for this transaction.
Whether the country-like address signal is available for this transaction.
Whether the distance/location signal is available for this transaction.
Business-level description of the distance/location pattern.
Purchaser email domain.
Recipient email domain, if available.
Device type such as desktop or mobile.
Device information string where available.
Proxy or network signal such as transparent, anonymous, or hidden proxy.
Operating system / OS version string.
Browser / browser version string.
Screen resolution, for example 1920x1080.
Whether any identity/device information is available.
